description: Governed stack packs for C.A.S.A stack composition.

presets.name: Web SaaS
presets.summary: React web app, Fastify API, Postgres persistence and web security baseline.

presets.name: Mobile App
presets.summary: Expo mobile app, API backend, Supabase client and web/API security baseline.

presets.name: Desktop App
presets.summary: Tauri desktop app with a React UI and SQLite/Prisma persistence.

presets.name: Filament Admin
presets.summary: Laravel admin panel with Filament, database persistence and Laravel security review points.

presets.name: AI Fullstack
presets.summary: Web SaaS preset with OpenRouter model routing.

packs.name: React App Foundation
packs.summary: React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind, Radix primitives, Lucide, TanStack Query/Table and Vitest.
questions: Which routes are public, authenticated and admin-only?
Which design system tokens are authoritative?
Which screens need loading, empty and error states first?

packs.name: Expo Mobile App Foundation
packs.summary: Expo, React Native, Expo Router, NativeWind, secure storage and mobile test baseline.
questions: Which data must never be stored in local device storage?
Which flows must work offline or recover after reconnect?
Which permissions require platform-specific explanation?

packs.name: Tauri Desktop App Foundation
packs.summary: Tauri desktop shell with React integration and desktop permission review points.
questions: Which native APIs are required and why?
Which filesystem or shell permissions are allowed?
How will builds be signed and distributed?

packs.name: Fastify API Foundation
packs.summary: Fastify API with CORS, rate limiting, Swagger/OpenAPI, Zod validation and Vitest.
questions: Which endpoints are public by design?
Which auth context is required at the boundary?
Which API contracts must be generated before implementation?

packs.name: NestJS API Foundation
packs.summary: NestJS API with config, validation, Swagger/OpenAPI and Jest test baseline.
questions: Which modules own domain logic?
Which guards are required before public routes ship?
Which contracts must be exported for clients?

packs.name: Postgres with Prisma
packs.summary: PostgreSQL access through Prisma, migrations and generated client.
questions: Which tenant boundary must every query preserve?
Which migrations are destructive or need rollback planning?
Which sensitive columns need ownership documentation?

packs.name: SQLite with Prisma
packs.summary: SQLite access through Prisma for local-first or desktop apps.
questions: Which local data must be encrypted or avoided?
How will sync conflicts be resolved?
Which migration rollback path is acceptable?

packs.name: Supabase Client
packs.summary: Supabase client integration with tenant and RLS review points.
questions: Which tables require row-level security before release?
Which keys are public publishable keys vs server-only keys?
Which policies must be tested with negative cases?

packs.name: Laravel PostgreSQL Baseline
packs.summary: Laravel/PostgreSQL persistence review pack for migrations, policies and seed safety.
questions: Which models require policies before Filament resources are exposed?
Which migrations need rollback and backup planning?
Which seed data is safe outside local development?

packs.name: Web and API Security Baseline
packs.summary: Validation, headers, rate limits, JWT/JWK handling and API authorization review.
questions: Which endpoints are unauthenticated by design?
Which claims or roles authorize sensitive actions?
Which negative authorization tests are mandatory?

packs.name: Desktop Security Baseline
packs.summary: Desktop permissions, local secret storage, signing and update channel review.
questions: Which OS permissions are strictly necessary?
Where are local credentials stored?
How are updates signed and verified?

packs.name: Laravel Security Baseline
packs.summary: Laravel policies, gates, middleware, CSRF/session posture and admin authorization review.
questions: Which users can access each Filament panel?
Which model policies block unauthorized resource actions?
Which admin actions need audit logs?

packs.name: Filament PHP Admin Panel
packs.summary: Filament admin panel pack for Laravel projects.
manual_steps: Run the Filament installer command required by the selected Filament major version.
Create policies before exposing sensitive resources.
Add audit logging for destructive admin actions.
questions: Which panel users and roles exist?
Which resources are read-only at first?
Which destructive actions need confirmation and audit evidence?

packs.name: OpenRouter Model Router
packs.summary: OpenRouter API configuration using environment variables, model selection and cost guardrails.
questions: Which model is the default and which models are allowed as fallback?
Where are API keys stored in each environment?
What request logging is safe without exposing prompts or secrets?
What budget, rate limit and timeout protect the integration?
